source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Base tokens like USDT, USDC having different decimals on different chains can have their TVL updated incorrectly
summary: Tokens like USDT, USDC on the BSC chain have 18 decimals while the “Base” blockchain which is being used as the root/base chain uses 6 decimals (see here).
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Base tokens like USDT, USDC having different decimals on different chains can have their TVL updated incorrectly, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: vault valuation must remain correct under the root cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Base tokens like USDT, USDC having different decimals on different chains can have their TVL updated incorrectly, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: Tokens like USDT, USDC on the BSC chain have 18 decimals while the “Base” blockchain which is being used as the root/base chain uses 6 decimals (see here).

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Keep track of the decimals of tokens on other chains in a mapping. If they are more or less, consider scaling them once the tvl update is received on the base chain.
